data.prompt:
You are Archon Horizon's Horizon agent. You turn the blueprints into checked Lean: you pick the most valuable next piece of formalization and carry it as far as you can in one session. You receive the same workspace context Ground saw — roadmap, inbox, memory, blueprint summary, and any Ground orientation — but you choose the strategy from the live Lean state.

You have broad freedom at the workspace level. You may change the route, fill infrastructure gaps, refactor proof APIs, edit blueprint material, update roadmap comments, retrieve references, delegate to subagents, and write local helper scripts/tools when that helps. The one hard rule is hygiene: stay within the write scope and shared `references/`, keep changes semantic, and leave no stray scratch files. Prefer ambitious progress over defensive avoidance.

Operational norms:
- Use the Lean LSP MCP for tight proof feedback, then verify with the narrowest faithful Lean/lake check.
- Use skills as needed for Lean checks, inbox coordination, commits, references, and subagents.
- Use `"$HORIZON_BIN"` for Horizon CLI actions; write inbox/task/roadmap comments with `--author horizon`.
- Commit coherent progress yourself with `"$HORIZON_BIN" commit -m "<math-first message>" <files>`.
- Record durable dead ends as memory and explain important route changes in your final report.

# Skills
Invoke the Horizon CLI via the `$HORIZON_BIN` env var (an absolute path), e.g. `"$HORIZON_BIN" inbox list --json` — a bare `horizon` may not be on your shell's PATH.
Most `horizon` commands accept `--json`; use it for AI-friendly output when reading state or ids.
Capability know-how lives in skill files under the workspace `.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md` (Claude Code surfaces them automatically; on other engines read the files directly). Consult the relevant one before acting (exact inbox verbs, lean checks, blueprint conventions, the dependency DAG):
- blueprint-conventions — The LaTeX-subset blueprint format and house style — environments, \uses/\lean/\leanok/\mathlibok/\notready, the math-purity rule, and complete (not sketched) proofs.
- horizon-commit — Record your own work in the workspace ledger with semantic commits via `horizon commit` — small, meaningful, math-first messages; safe under concurrent runs.
- horizon-inbox — Read and act on the Archon Horizon inbox — list/filter agent-ready items, understand labels/kinds, comment, archive, create, and message other projects via the `horizon inbox` CLI.
- horizon-waiting — How to wait for slow work (builds, subagents, monitors) inside a one-shot session — block in the foreground and get the result before you report; you are NEVER re-invoked on completion.
- lean-check — Build and check Lean in this workspace and read the errors — use Lean LSP MCP for fast proof-writing feedback, then run faithful kernel verification with `lake build` / `lake env lean`.
- leandag — Consult and refresh the blueprint dependency DAG (leandag) — declaration dependencies, cones, proved status, isolated/dangling nodes, verified union/intersection across projects — via `horizon leandag` and the cached DAG files.
- leansearch — Find existing lemmas/definitions before proving — the local `horizon search` index plus the Lean LSP MCP search tools (LeanSearch/Loogle/state-search/hammer-premise). Reach for search before writing any nontrivial proof; the premise you need often already exists in mathlib.
- project-git — How git works in an Archon Horizon workspace — root commits include scoped project files plus shared state, while each project also has an out-of-tree VCS journal for detailed per-round history.
- references — The shared workspace-root references/ library and its manifest.yaml — how to find, read, and cite original sources with \source{...}. Read the source before you cite it (never speculate); anchor TeX sources by slug, and for PDF-only sources extract the LaTeX first (vision, never OCR).
- subagents — How the Ground agent delegates to native subagents to divide review/upkeep work — spawning by name, read-only enforcement, model tiers, and the dynamic descriptor roster.

Workspace: JacobianChallenge
Project: Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge at MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge
Write scope — you may edit ONLY paths matching these globs; treat everything else as read-only:
- MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/**
- references/**
Read scope: the entire workspace (cross-project reads are allowed).
Log/artifact directory: .archon-horizon/runs/0014/sessions/0003-horizon-T14

# Subagents
You have native subagents installed for your engine, compiled at run start from the descriptors in `.archon-horizon/subagents/<name>.md` (Claude reads `.claude/agents/`, Codex reads `.codex/agents/`):
- blueprint — Own one assigned blueprint slice end-to-end — keep it pure math with complete proofs, cite references with \source{...} anchors, hold a 1-to-1 correspondence with the Lean, and make its dependency cone finite and clean.
- debug — Diagnose and repair workspace setup/build/environment failures during a run — missing toolchain, broken lakefile, MCP/config/env problems, obscure non-Lean-syntax errors — applying safe fixes or reporting a precise diagnosis.
- janitor — Workspace-hygiene auditor and tidier — keeps the workspace and each subproject navigable, READMEs/roadmap concise and current, files findable, and the inbox from overflowing (capping memory tags, closing what's done).
- page-transcriber — Transcribe a small PDF page range (max 5 pages) into faithful per-page LaTeX using AI VISION on rendered page images (never OCR), each page self-contained with the operators/macros it declares.
- reference-retriever — Fetch original math references into workspace-root references/, register them in references/manifest.yaml, and when needed transcribe PDF page ranges into page-level LaTeX files for blueprint \source{...} anchors.
- work-reviewer [read-only] — Fresh-context review of the previous Horizon agent's work on a task — starts from its git diff, its report, and the task it was given, but is free to dig deeper — then judges whether the work is converging and files concise inbox issues/memory for stuck points and errors.

Delegate by spawning a subagent **by name** through your engine's own subagent mechanism, giving it a focused directive: the slice/scope and the project it applies to. Spawn several in parallel when the work divides cleanly, then wait for and reconcile their reports. Read-only subagents are sandboxed off source edits and report through the `horizon inbox` CLI. You decide whether a subagent fits — use them when they help, skip them when they don't. Each subagent's descriptor pins its own model, usually a cheaper one for mechanical work: prefer delegating routine checks (diff review, reference lookup, lint) to those cheaper subagents and keep your own (more expensive) context for the hard reasoning. To add or change a subagent, edit its descriptor under `.archon-horizon/subagents/` — it recompiles on the next run.

This was saved as `recommendation.md` by the last Ground session. Treat it as lightweight orientation: useful files, recent context, and possible consistency concerns. It is not a plan, command, or boundary; use your own judgment from the live Lean state.

# Opened inbox
- I-0118 issue (by=horizon, to=horizon, labels=agent-ready, projects=Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge): QuotScheme statement is unprovable-as-pinned: properness alone is not the Nitsure theorem The pinned AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.QuotScheme (QuotFunctorDef.lean, final theorem) hypothesizes only [IsProper pi] [LocallyOfFiniteType pi] with arbitrary quasi-coherent L and arbitrary E, but the source theorem ([Nitsure] Sec. 5, thm:quot_representable) requires pi projective, L relatively very ample, E coherent. For merely proper X the Quot/Hilbert functor is in general only an algebraic space (Hironaka's smooth proper non-projective 3-fold is the standard counterexample family), so the pinned statement is false-or-open, not the cited theorem. Same trap genus as the old Grassmannian.representable sk…

# Memory
- **tangentSpaceIso (A.3.iii) is NOT directly provable — critical path runs through the relative Pic functor, and a naive FGA rewire is a FALSE-axiom trap.**

- `Pic0.tangentSpaceIso` (`Pic0AbelianVariety.lean`) cannot be proven against the current foundation: `picSharp` in `FGAPicRepresentability.lean` is `Classical.choice` of a `⟨sorry⟩` `Nonempty` — a fully opaque functor. No H¹ comparison can attach to it. Do not re-probe the sorry directly.
- **Critical path** (in order): (1) ~~real relative Pic presheaf~~ DONE session 0015 (`PicSharp.relPresheaf` in `RelPicFunctor.lean`, `H_T`-coset carrier, axiom-clean); (2) étale sheafification target — Mathlib v4.31 has NO étale Grothendieck topology on schemes; either parametrize or use Kleiman §2 Thm 2.5: for C/k with a **k-rational section**, `Pic^♯(T) → Pic^♯_ét(T)` is already bijective, so representability can be stated against `relPresheaf` directly under a section hypothesis; (3) rewire FGA `picSharp` := (sheafified) relative functor — universe bump `Type u → Type (u+1)` in `HasPicSharp`/`HasPicScheme` is viable (`Functor.RepresentableBy` is universe-polymorphic; `Equiv` is cross-universe); (4) `Pic(C_ε) ≅ H¹(C_ε, O^×)` cocycle classification + truncated exponential split sequence `0 → O_C → O^×_{C_ε} → O^×_C → 1` for the kernel = `H¹(C,O_C)` computation (Kleiman Thm 5.11) — none of this exists yet, largest remaining block.
- **TRAP**: wiring `picSharp := PicSharp.presheaf` (the absolute functor, iso-class carrier) would make `PicSharpRepresentable`'s sorry a mathematically FALSE statement (absolute `Pic(C×T)` is not representable — it differs from `Hom(T, Pic)` by `Pic(T)`). Any rewire must use the relative (and in general sheafified) functor.
- Proof-pattern notes: the `Quotient.lift`/`Quotient.sound` + iso-chain style (pullbackCongr/pullbackId/pullbackComp `.app`, D4′ `pullbackTensorIsoOfLocallyTrivial`, `pullback_pullback_eq`) compiled essentially first-try — no v4.31 transparency walls in this corner. `pullback.lift_fst/snd` are NOT simp (only `@[reassoc]`); pass them + their `_assoc` forms to `simp` explicitly after `pullback.hom_ext`.
- Milne 3.1/3.2 extension chain: "CodimOneFree ⇒ extension to a complete target" is FALSE — never re-add it; the honest chain and the reusable Mathlib extension recipe.

- **Do not reintroduce** an `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth`-style statement (codim-1-free rational map to a complete variety extends): counterexample $\mathbb P^2 \dashrightarrow \mathbb P^1$ away from a point (indeterminacy = one codim-2 point, no regular extension; blow-up needed). Removed in run-0006 session 0015 after its `sorry` sat unclosable for ~25 iterations. A Weil-style codim-≥2 extension is true only for **group-variety** targets, and Milne's route never needs it.
- **Honest chain** (all in `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean` + `Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean`): Milne 3.1 = `indeterminacy_codimGe2_of_smooth_of_complete` (proved, valuative); Milne 3.3 (`indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme`, the only remaining sorry) must assert the codim-1 witness lies **in $Z(f)$** (without that conjunct the disjunct is nearly vacuous and cannot force emptiness); 3.3 + 3.1 ⇒ $Z(f)=\emptyset$ (`av_indeterminacyLocus_eq_empty`); then extension. **Stacks 0AVF / Auslander–Buchsbaum depth-≥2 is NOT needed anywhere in this chain** (supersedes older notes listing 0AVF as a Milne-3.1 blocker).
- **Recipe: extension from empty indeterminacy** (`existsUnique_hom_of_indeterminacyLocus_eq_empty`, reduced X / separated Y): witness `X.topIso.inv ≫ X.homOfLE hdom.ge ≫ f.toPartialMap.hom` where `hdom : f.toPartialMap.domain = ⊤` (`toPartialMap.domain = f.domain` is `rfl`); rational-class equality via `PartialMap.toRationalMap_eq_iff.mpr ⟨⊤, …⟩` + `simp only [PartialMap.restrict_hom, Scheme.Hom.toPartialMap, Scheme.topIso_hom, Scheme.ι_toIso_inv_assoc, Scheme.homOfLE_homOfLE_assoc]`; uniqueness via `hom_ext_of_toRationalMap_eq` (= `PartialMap.toRationalMap_eq_iff.mp` + `Opens.isDominant_ι` + `ext_of_isDominant`, positional arg — the parser rejects `(ι := …)` named form here). Both lemmas need `set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false` BEFORE the docstring (`(restrict …).domain` / `Hom.toPartialMap` defeq walls).
- **Standing lesson**: before attacking a long-lived typed `sorry`, sanity-check the statement for truth (hunt small counterexamples); a false pinned statement silently blocks a whole cone and can smuggle wrong `\uses` (here it dragged a phantom `cor:regular_cohen_macaulay`/0AVF dependency into the blueprint for ~30 iterations).
- **FGA picSharp rewire LANDED (run 0008): route-(b) conventions + two standing caveats.**

State after the rewire (`Picard/FGAPicRepresentability.lean`, **4 real `sorry`s**, all TRUE statements — Ground-verified run 0008 reconcile):

- `picSharp C := PicSharp.relPresheaf C ⋙ forget AddCommGrpCat` (`Type (u+1)`-valued, REAL). `Scheme.HasRationalPoint C` = Nonempty section subtype of `C.hom`; the ONE genuine FGA sorry is `instHasPicScheme [HasRationalPoint C]`. The other three are typed-sorry carriers: `PicSchemeLocallyOfFiniteType` (Kleiman §4 `th:main`(1), true under the rational point) and the pre-existing Quot-blocked `HasDivFunctor` / `HasAbelMap`.
- `instPicSharpRepresentable` proved by `choose_spec`; `groupSchemeStructure : CommGrpObj (PicScheme C)` proved via `CommGrpObj.ofRepresentableBy` + a `Multiplicative.ofAdd` NatIso (`picSharpCommGrpForgetIso`, naturality is `rfl` — no defeq walls anywhere in this file).
- Consumers: quantify over `[HasPicScheme C]` (synthesized from `[HasRationalPoint C]`) — `degree`/`kPoints_iff_kerDegree` in `IdentityComponent.lean` now carry it. `Pic0Scheme C := GroupScheme.IdentityComponent (PicScheme C)` is now a real def (axiom-clean), gated on `PicSchemeLocallyOfFiniteType`.

**Caveat 1 — pointless curves:** `JacobianWitness` (`Jacobian.lean`) requires the abelian variety $J$ to exist UNconditionally, but route (b) only builds `PicScheme` for pointed $C$. For a genus-$\ge 1$ curve without a rational point the eventual `picardJacobianWitness` proof needs Galois/étale descent from a finite extension where $C$ gains a point (or a pointed restatement of the north star). Not blocking anything now — record before anyone tries to close `picardJacobianWitness` directly.

**Caveat 2 — `smoothProperQuotient` statement is WEAKER than Kleiman lm:qt and unprovable as stated:** Mathlib v4.31 has NO quasi-projectivity vocabulary, so hypothesis (2) of lm:qt is missing from the Lean statement; without it the statement is FALSE (Hironaka-type free ℤ/2 action on a smooth proper non-projective 3-fold: smooth proper equivalence relation, étale-quotient sheaf a non-scheme algebraic space). The former GLOBAL `instHasSmoothProperQuotient ⟨sorry⟩` (every presheaf representable!) was deleted this run (Ground-confirmed gone); the class is a use-site hypothesis only. Do NOT reintroduce a global instance, and do NOT try to prove the theorem as stated.
- **`pullback_preservesFiniteLimits` (FBC leaf, `CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional.lean:186`) is NOT a cheap adjointness win — Ground's recommendation had the variance backwards. Route analysis (session 0013), do not re-derive.**

- The recommendation claimed "flat `g^*` is a left adjoint, so left-exactness follows from adjointness" — **false**: left adjoints preserve COLIMITS. Left-exactness of flat pullback is genuine flatness content, checked on stalks.

data.content:
==================== I-0118 issue 
QuotScheme statement is unprovable-as-pinned: properness alone is not the Nitsure theorem

The pinned AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.QuotScheme (QuotFunctorDef.lean, final theorem) hypothesizes only [IsProper pi] [LocallyOfFiniteType pi] with arbitrary quasi-coherent L and arbitrary E, but the source theorem ([Nitsure] Sec. 5, thm:quot_representable) requires pi projective, L relatively very ample, E coherent. For merely proper X the Quot/Hilbert functor is in general only an algebraic space (Hironaka's smooth proper non-projective 3-fold is the standard counterexample family), so the pinned statement is false-or-open, not the cited theorem. Same trap genus as the old Grassmannian.representable skeleton corrected this session (GrassmannianRepresentability.lean, thm:grassmannian_representable NOTE). Next action before attacking this sorry: design the projectivity / relative-very-ampleness encoding (absent from Mathlib v4.31, see I-0109 ampleness gap), restate faithfully, then prove. Do NOT close the sorry as stated.
--- comment C-0001 by ground
**Blueprint side reconciled (session 0109-ground).** The node `thm:quot_representable` (`Picard_QuotScheme.tex:4374`) was marked `\leanok` despite (a) its Lean target `QuotScheme` being an unproved `sorry` and (b) the statement mismatch you flag. I set it to `\notready` with an inline comment recording the mismatch, so the DAG no longer claims the weak/false Lean signature is verified.

- The **blueprint statement is correct** (projective $\pi$, relatively very ample $L$, coherent $E$ — matches Nitsure §5). The defect is purely on the Lean side: `QuotScheme` hypothesizes only `[IsProper] [LocallyOfFiniteType]` over arbitrary quasi-coherent $L,E$.
- Per memory `I-0074` caveat 2, Mathlib v4.31 has no projectivity/very-ampleness vocabulary, so the Lean cannot yet be restated to match — the honest move is to leave `QuotScheme` unproved (or re-scope it) rather than prove the false pinned form.
--- comment C-0002 by human
**Restatement design settled** (T12 ultracode session triage; Mathlib v4.31 vocabulary hunt done — NO IsProjective/QuasiProjective morphism class, NO VeryAmple/IsAmple for sheaves, NO relative Proj or $\mathbb{P}^n_S$, NO $\mathcal{O}(1)$ as a sheaf of modules anywhere in Mathlib).

Recommended faithful encoding (a): build the missing vocabulary block project-side —
- $\mathbb{P}(n;S) :=$ `pullback (terminal.from S) (terminal.from (Proj (MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin n) ℤ)))`, in the style of Mathlib's `AffineSpace` (`AffineSpace.lean:44`). Properness of the structural map comes from Mathlib's `IsProper (Proj.toSpecZero 𝒜)` (`ProjectiveSpectrum/Proper.lean:368`) + base change.
- Serre twist $\mathcal{O}(1)$ as a `SheafOfModules` on `Proj 𝒜`, glued over the `awayι`/basic-open affine cover with transitions $t_i/t_j$ — the project's `GlueDescent`/`GrassmannianZariskiSheaf` module-gluing engine is the substrate.
- Then restate: `theorem QuotScheme … (hE : E.IsFinitePresentation) (emb : ∃ n (i : X ⟶ ℙ(n;S)), IsClosedImmersion i ∧ i ≫ proj = π ∧ Nonempty (L ≅ i^* 𝒪(1)))` and flip `thm:quot_representable` from \notready.

Fallback encoding (c) for the sole Route-A consumer ($S = \operatorname{Spec} k$): very-ampleness through the project's own `SectionGradedRing` $\bigoplus_m \Gamma(X, L^{\otimes m})$ + Mathlib `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` (`ProjectiveSpectrum/Basic.lean:439`): hypothesis = the canonical $X \to \operatorname{Proj}$ of the section ring is a closed immersion + degree-1 generation/finite type.

Encoding (b) (a class packaging the Nitsure-consumed consequences — m-regularity bounds, pushforward local freeness) is REJECTED as the public statement: conclusion-carrier of the `HasSmoothProperQuotient` genus, statement-drift magnet; acceptable only as a use-site hypothesis.

Next action: dedicated vocabulary-build session for $\mathbb{P}(n;S)$ + $\mathcal{O}(1)$, then the restatement. Until then `Scheme.QuotScheme` (QuotFunctorDef.lean:753) keeps its sorry.
==================== I-0109 memory 
**T12 r-current: hilbertPolynomial headline def CLOSED (real definition) — updates the 'QuotScheme 8 sorries' triage of I-0089.**

- New file `Picard/HilbertPolynomial.lean` (~230 LOC, axiom-clean `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`, LSP + full `lake build` green, 8647 jobs): `Scheme.hilbertPolynomial` is now REAL — the unique `Φ ∈ ℚ[λ]` eventually matching the graded Hilbert function `m ↦ dim_{κ(s)} Γ(X_s, F_s ⊗ L_s^{⊗m})` (`Scheme.hilbertFunction`), junk `0` outside the regime. Fibre = Mathlib `Scheme.Hom.fiber`; restrictions via `Scheme.Modules.pullback (π.fiberι s)` (`Hom.fiberModule`); twists via `SectionGradedRing.moduleTensorPow`; κ(s)-structure = `Module.compHom` along `Hom.fiberResidueMap` (`ΓSpecIso.inv ≫ (fiberToSpecResidueField).appTop`) — all compiled essentially first-try, no defeq walls.

14	# Affine space
15	
16	## Main definitions
17	
18	- `AlgebraicGeometry.AffineSpace`: `𝔸(n; S)` is the affine `n`-space over `S`.
19	- `AlgebraicGeometry.AffineSpace.coord`: The standard coordinate functions on the affine space.
20	- `AlgebraicGeometry.AffineSpace.homOfVector`:
21	  The morphism `X ⟶ 𝔸(n; S)` given by a `X ⟶ S` and a choice of `n`-coordinate functions.
22	- `AlgebraicGeometry.AffineSpace.homOverEquiv`:
23	  `S`-morphisms into `Spec 𝔸(n; S)` are equivalent to the choice of `n` global sections.
24	- `AlgebraicGeometry.AffineSpace.SpecIso`: `𝔸(n; Spec R) ≅ Spec R[n]`
25	
26	-/
27	
28	@[expose] public section
29	
30	open CategoryTheory Limits MvPolynomial
31	
32	noncomputable section
33	
34	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
35	
36	universe u
37	
38	variable (n : Type u) (S : Scheme.{u})
39	
40	local notation3 "ℤ[" n "]" => CommRingCat.of (MvPolynomial n (ULift ℤ))
41	
42	/-- `𝔸(n; S)` is the affine `n`-space over `S`.
43	Note that `n : Type u` is an arbitrary index type (e.g. `ULift.{u} (Fin m)`). -/
44	def AffineSpace (n : Type u) (S : Scheme.{u}) : Scheme.{u} :=
45	  pullback (terminal.from S) (terminal.from (Spec ℤ[n]))
46	
47	namespace AffineSpace
48	
49	/-- `𝔸(n; S)` is the affine `n`-space over `S`. -/
50	scoped[AlgebraicGeometry] notation "𝔸(" n "; " S ")" => AffineSpace n S
51	
52	variable {n} in
53	lemma of_mvPolynomial_int_ext {R} {f g : ℤ[n] ⟶ R} (h : ∀ i, f (.X i) = g (.X i)) : f = g := by
54	  suffices f.hom.comp (MvPolynomial.mapEquiv _ ULift.ringEquiv.symm).toRingHom =
55	      g.hom.comp (MvPolynomial.mapEquiv _ ULift.ringEquiv.symm).toRingHom by
56	    ext x
57	    · obtain ⟨x⟩ := x
58	      simpa [-map_intCast, -eq_intCast] using! DFunLike.congr_fun this (C x)
59	    · simpa [-map_intCast, -eq_intCast] using! DFunLike.congr_fun this (X x)
60	  ext1
61	  · exact RingHom.ext_int _ _
62	  · simpa using! h _
63	
64	
65	@[simps -isSimp]
66	instance over : 𝔸(n; S).CanonicallyOver S where
67	  hom := pullback.fst _ _
68	
69	/-- The map from the affine `n`-space over `S` to the integral model `Spec ℤ[n]`. -/
70	def toSpecMvPoly : 𝔸(n; S) ⟶ Spec ℤ[n] := pullback.snd _ _
71	
72	set_option backward.defeqAttrib.useBackward true in
73	/--
74	Morphisms into `Spec ℤ[n]` are equivalent the choice of `n` global sections.
75	Use `homOverEquiv` instead.
76	-/
77	@[simps]
78	def toSpecMvPolyIntEquiv {X : Scheme.{u}} : (X ⟶ Spec ℤ[n]) ≃ (n → Γ(X, ⊤)) where
79	  toFun f i := f.appTop ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso ℤ[n]).inv (.X i))
80	  invFun v := X.toSpecΓ ≫ Spec.map
81	    (CommRingCat.ofHom (MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom ((algebraMap ℤ _).comp ULift.ringEquiv.toRingHom) v))
82	  left_inv f := by
83	    apply (ΓSpec.adjunction.homEquiv _ _).symm.injective
84	    apply Quiver.Hom.unop_inj
85	    rw [Adjunction.homEquiv_symm_apply, Adjunction.homEquiv_symm_apply]
86	    dsimp
87	    simp only [Scheme.toSpecΓ_appTop, Scheme.ΓSpecIso_naturality, Iso.inv_hom_id_assoc]
88	    apply of_mvPolynomial_int_ext
89	    intro i
90	    rw [ConcreteCategory.hom_ofHom, coe_eval₂Hom, eval₂_X]
91	    rfl
92	  right_inv v := by ext; simp
93	
94	lemma toSpecMvPolyIntEquiv_comp {X Y : Scheme} (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : Y ⟶ Spec ℤ[n]) (i) :
95	    toSpecMvPolyIntEquiv n (f ≫ g) i = f.appTop (toSpecMvPolyIntEquiv n g i) := rfl
96	
97	variable {n} in
98	/-- The standard coordinates of `𝔸(n; S)`. -/
99	def coord (i : n) : Γ(𝔸(n; S), ⊤) := toSpecMvPolyIntEquiv _ (toSpecMvPoly n S) i
100	
101	section homOfVector
102	
103	variable {n S}
104	
105	/-- The morphism `X ⟶ 𝔸(n; S)` given by a `X ⟶ S` and a choice of `n`-coordinate functions. -/
106	def homOfVector {X : Scheme.{u}} (f : X ⟶ S) (v : n → Γ(X, ⊤)) : X ⟶ 𝔸(n; S) :=
107	  pullback.lift f ((toSpecMvPolyIntEquiv n).symm v) (by simp)
108	
109	variable {X : Scheme.{u}} (f : X ⟶ S) (v : n → Γ(X, ⊤))
110	
111	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
112	lemma homOfVector_over : homOfVector f v ≫ 𝔸(n; S) ↘ S = f :=
113	  pullback.lift_fst _ _ _
114	
115	@[reassoc]
116	lemma homOfVector_toSpecMvPoly :
117	    homOfVector f v ≫ toSpecMvPoly n S = (toSpecMvPolyIntEquiv n).symm v :=
118	  pullback.lift_snd _ _ _

@[simp]
lemma reindex_appTop_coord {n m : Type u} (i : m → n) (S : Scheme.{u}) (j : m) :
    (reindex i S).appTop (coord S j) = coord S (i j) :=
  homOfVector_appTop_coord _ _ _

@[simp]
lemma reindex_id : reindex id S = 𝟙 𝔸(n; S) := by
  ext1 <;> simp

@[simp, reassoc]
lemma reindex_comp {n₁ n₂ n₃ : Type u} (i : n₁ ⟶ n₂) (j : n₂ ⟶ n₃) (S : Scheme.{u}) :
    reindex (i ≫ j) S = reindex j S ≫ reindex i S := by
  ext k <;> simp

@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
lemma map_reindex {n₁ n₂ : Type u} (i : n₁ → n₂) {S T : Scheme.{u}} (f : S ⟶ T) :
    map n₂ f ≫ reindex i T = reindex i S ≫ map n₁ f := by
  apply hom_ext <;> simp

/-- The affine space as a functor. -/
@[simps]
def functor : (Type u)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Scheme.{u} ⥤ Scheme.{u} where
  obj n := { obj := AffineSpace n.unop, map := map n.unop, map_id := map_id, map_comp := map_comp }
  map {n m} i := { app := reindex i.unop, naturality := fun _ _ ↦ map_reindex i.unop }
  map_id n := by ext : 2; exact reindex_id _
  map_comp f g := by ext : 2; dsimp; exact reindex_comp _ _ _

end functorial
section instances

instance : IsAffineHom (𝔸(n; S) ↘ S) := MorphismProperty.pullback_fst _ _ inferInstance

instance : Surjective (𝔸(n; S) ↘ S) := MorphismProperty.pullback_fst _ _ <| by
  have := isIso_of_isTerminal specULiftZIsTerminal terminalIsTerminal (terminal.from _)
  rw [← terminal.comp_from (Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom C)),
    MorphismProperty.cancel_right_of_respectsIso (P := @Surjective)]
  exact ⟨MvPolynomial.comap_C_surjective⟩

instance [Finite n] : LocallyOfFinitePresentation (𝔸(n; S) ↘ S) :=
  MorphismProperty.pullback_fst _ _ <| by
  have := isIso_of_isTerminal specULiftZIsTerminal.{u} terminalIsTerminal (terminal.from _)
  rw [← terminal.comp_from (Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom C)),
    MorphismProperty.cancel_right_of_respectsIso (P := @LocallyOfFinitePresentation),
    HasRingHomProperty.Spec_iff (P := @LocallyOfFinitePresentation), RingHom.FinitePresentation]
  convert! (inferInstance : Algebra.FinitePresentation (ULift ℤ) ℤ[n])
  exact Algebra.algebra_ext _ _ fun _ ↦ rfl

lemma isOpenMap_over : IsOpenMap (𝔸(n; S) ↘ S) := by
  change topologically @IsOpenMap _
  wlog hS : ∃ R, S = Spec R
  · refine (IsZariskiLocalAtTarget.iff_of_openCover
      (P := topologically @IsOpenMap) S.affineCover).mpr ?_
    intro i
    have := this (n := n) (S.affineCover.X i) ⟨_, rfl⟩
    rwa [← (isPullback_map (n := n) (S.affineCover.f i)).isoPullback_hom_snd,
      MorphismProperty.cancel_left_of_respectsIso (P := topologically @IsOpenMap)] at this
  obtain ⟨R, rfl⟩ := hS
  rw [← MorphismProperty.cancel_left_of_respectsIso (P := topologically @IsOpenMap)
    (SpecIso n R).inv, SpecIso_inv_over]
  exact MvPolynomial.isOpenMap_comap_C

instance : GeometricallyIrreducible (𝔸(n; S) ↘ S) := by
  rw [geometricallyIrreducible_iff]
  introv K h
  apply ObjectProperty.prop_of_iso _
    ((h.isoIsPullback _ _ (isPullback_map _)) ≪≫ (SpecIso n (.of K))).symm
  infer_instance

instance [IrreducibleSpace S] : IrreducibleSpace 𝔸(n; S) :=
  GeometricallyIrreducible.irreducibleSpace (𝔸(n; S) ↘ S) (isOpenMap_over S)

instance : GeometricallyReduced (𝔸(n; S) ↘ S) := by
  rw [geometricallyReduced_iff]
  introv K h
  apply ObjectProperty.prop_of_iso _
    ((h.isoIsPullback _ _ (isPullback_map _)) ≪≫ (SpecIso n (.of K))).symm
  infer_instance

instance [h : IsReduced S] : IsReduced 𝔸(n; S) := by
  wlog hS : ∃ R, S = Spec R
  · rw [IsReduced.iff_of_openCover _ (S.affineCover.pullback₁ (𝔸(n; S) ↘ S))]
    intro i
    have : IsReduced 𝔸(n; S.affineCover.X i) := this _ ⟨_, rfl⟩
    exact isReduced_of_isOpenImmersion ((isPullback_map _).isoPullback.inv)
  obtain ⟨R, rfl⟩ := hS
  rw [affine_isReduced_iff] at h
  exact isReduced_of_isOpenImmersion (SpecIso n R).hom

instance : GeometricallyIntegral (𝔸(n; S) ↘ S) :=
  .of_geometricallyReduced_of_geometricallyIrreducible _
